prompt: Identical scene to the reference image — same dusty red-brown Australian construction site, same camera angle, same lighting, same background elements: timber pallets stacked at left, chain-link fence behind, orange traffic cones, wheelbarrow, gravel pile, workers and vehicles in the far background, same blue sky with wispy clouds, same harsh midday Australian sun. CRITICAL CHANGE: the person crouching behind the bush has been completely replaced by a dark blue-grey Colorbond steel portable toilet with 'SATURN' branding on the side panel, identical to the second reference image. The toilet is positioned exactly where the person and bush were — centre-left of frame, sitting on the red dirt. CRITICAL: The toilet must be rendered at FULL REALISTIC SCALE — a real portable toilet is approximately 2.3 metres tall and 1.2 metres wide, which is significantly LARGER than the small scraggly bush and much taller than the crouching person it replaces. The toilet should dominate the foreground and completely dwarf the bush behind it. Do not shrink the toilet to match the bush size — the toilet is a full-height structure that a standing adult walks into. The bush is no longer visible as the toilet is directly in front of it, fully covering and towering over where the person was crouching. A small cloud of red-brown dust is settling around the base of the toilet as if it just landed moments ago, with fine particles still suspended in the air near ground level. The toilet door faces camera-right, matching the reference toilet image orientation. Everything else in the scene is EXACTLY the same — same ground texture, same background objects in the same positions, same lighting direction, same sky. The only difference from the reference photo is the person and bush have been replaced by the Saturn portable toilet with settling dust. Shot from the same distance and angle as the reference — approximately 8 metres, eye level, 35mm lens, f/5.6, deep focus. Natural harsh Australian midday sunlight. Canon EOS R5 colour science.
